exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Fly
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your head, upper back and glutes are supported (supine on an incline bench) and place your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help rack the dumbbells and press them overhead so your arms are extended above your chest, palms facing each other (neutral grip); keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement; avoid excessive lower-back arching.

Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells in a wide, controlled arc out to your sides until you feel a stretch in the upper chest—stop when your elbows are roughly in line with the bench or slightly below shoulder level.
Keep the elbows fixed at the same slight bend throughout the descent; do not let the elbows drop or the wrists bend excessively.
Exhale and bring the dumbbells back together over your chest by squeezing the pectorals, following the same arc path used on the way down.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights close together (but avoid slamming them), then repeat for the desired number of reps.
Use a lighter weight if you feel shoulder joint pain; focus on controlled range of motion and chest contraction rather than heavy load.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a wide-arc dumbbell fly to emphasize the upper portion of the chest; movement stretches the chest as you lower and contracts the pectorals as you bring the weights back together. Keep a slight bend in the elbows and move in a controlled arc to avoid shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Cable Middle Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered stance (one foot slightly forward) with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or palms-facing grip and extend your arms out to the sides so there is tension on the cables; keep a small, constant bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades and keep the shoulders down; brace your core to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and bring the handles together in front of your chest in a wide arc, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Inhale and slowly reverse the motion with control, allowing the arms to return to the starting position until you feel a gentle stretch in the chest.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding torso leaning, shoulder shrugging, or locking the elbows.
exercises.description: Standing bilateral cable fly performed with the pulleys set at mid-chest height; the exercise horizontally adducts the arms in a controlled arc to load and contract the pectorals. A staggered stance and slight elbow bend are used to stabilize and protect the joints while the chest performs the work.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back (supine on an incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability; bring the dumbbells up to shoulder height with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly (pinch the shoulder blades together) to stabilize the chest and protect the shoulders.
Begin the repetition by slowly lowering the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight elbow bend throughout; lower until you feel a comfortable stretch in the upper chest.
Keep the movement controlled and avoid dropping the weights below the plane of the chest or allowing the elbows to fully lock or flare excessively.
Drive the dumbbells back up by horizontally adducting the shoulders (bringing the arms together) until they meet or nearly meet above the sternum; exhale as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then inhale and repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a neutral neck and avoid shrugging the shoulders; use a weight that allows strict form and full control through both eccentric and concentric phases.
exercises.description: Performed lying back on an inclined bench, the dumbbell incline fly isolates the upper portion of the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from open to together while maintaining a slight elbow bend. It emphasizes pectoral horizontal adduction with shoulder stabilization from the delts and grip support from the forearms.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered or shoulder-width stance with knees soft and core braced (slight forward lean at the hips).
Reach down and grab each handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip, palms facing in or up, elbows slightly bent and fixed in that position throughout the movement.
Begin with the arms out to the sides and low (handles near hips or knees) and inhale.
Exhale and pull the handles upward and inward in a wide arc, keeping the elbows slightly bent, bringing the hands together or in front of the chest/upper sternum.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze the pectorals while avoiding excessive shoulder shrugging.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, allowing the cables to return the arms to the start position while maintaining tension in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions and re-rack safely; keep torso stable and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly is a low-to-high cable chest fly that targets the pectorals by sweeping the arms upward and together from low pulley positions; performed standing with a slight forward lean and a soft bend in the elbows to emphasize the chest contraction.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place a pair of dumbbells nearby.
Sit on the bench with the dumbbells resting on your thighs, feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help lift the dumbbells as you lie back so the weights are beside your upper chest; hold them with a neutral or slightly supinated grip.
Retract your shoulder blades and press your upper back into the bench so your chest is proud (maintain a stable supine position on the incline).
Plant your feet and brace your core to avoid excessive arching of the spine.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together in a controlled path until your arms are almost fully extended (do not lock the elbows).
Exhale as you press up, pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position at the upper chest level.
Keep the elbows at a moderate angle (not flared excessively) and the wrists neutral throughout the movement.
Repeat for desired repetitions and use a spotter for heavy sets or if you cannot safely return the weights to your thighs/stand them up.
exercises.description: The dumbbell incline bench press is performed lying supine on an incline bench and pressing dumbbells upward to emphasize the upper portion of the chest. It builds strength and mass in the upper pectorals while also recruiting the front delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
Select an appropriate weight and sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest up and shoulders down and back.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and position your upper arms roughly parallel to the floor with a small bend in the elbows.
Start with the handles out to the sides so your chest is stretched but shoulders are not overstretched; maintain a stable torso against the back pad.
Exhale and drive the handles together in a smooth horizontal arc by contracting the pectorals, bringing your hands close together or slightly in front of the chest.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, squeezing the chest while keeping elbows slightly bent and avoiding shoulder shrugging.
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to return to the start position until you feel a controlled stretch in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding leaning forward or using momentum to move the weight.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly that brings the arms together in a horizontal arc to isolate and contract the pectoral muscles. Performed on a lever/pec-deck machine with the chest supported and elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Chest Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest height and select an appropriate weight; sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor and the bench back supporting your spine.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest height and select an appropriate weight; sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor and the bench back supporting your spine.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or slight supinated grip and take one step forward so there is tension in the cables; arms should start out to the sides with a small bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly and keep your chest up; maintain a stable, upright seated position throughout the movement.
Exhale as you draw both handles together in a wide arc in front of your chest, keeping the elbows slightly bent and fixed in that angle; aim to have the hands meet or come close at midline.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze the pectorals without shrugging the shoulders or stepping forward with the torso.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position in a controlled manner, allowing the chest to stretch but keeping tension on the cables.
Keep the movement driven by the chest—avoid using momentum, excessive elbow extension, or large torso lean; if balance is an issue, press your back into the bench.
Perform the desired number of reps with consistent form, resetting scapular position between reps if needed and adjusting weight if you cannot maintain control.
exercises.description: Seated cable chest fly is an isolation movement performed on dual cable pulleys, bringing the arms together in a horizontal arc to emphasize the pectorals. It uses a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to maximize chest contraction while seated upright.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
instructions: Step 1: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and elbows slightly bent.
Step 2: Brace your core and gently retract and depress your shoulder blades to stabilize the shoulder girdle throughout the movement.

Step 3: Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight bend in your elbows and stopping when your upper arms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the chest (avoid dropping below bench level).

Step 4: Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, then exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc by contracting the pectorals until the weights meet or are close together over your sternum.
Step 5: Maintain controlled tempo (no bouncing), keep elbows fixed at the same angle, and avoid locking the arms at the top; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Lying flat on a bench while holding dumbbells, the dumbbell fly opens the arms in a wide arc and brings them together to isolate and stretch the chest (pectorals). Movement emphasizes a controlled, arcing motion with a slight bend in the elbows to protect the shoulder joint.

Exhale and drive the handles together in a smooth horizontal arc by contracting the pectorals, bringing your hands close together or slightly in front of the chest.

exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline bench).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip; bring the dumbbells to shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°) and wrists stacked over elbows.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the elbows under the wrists (avoid excessive flaring).
Exhale and press both dumbbells up and slightly together until your arms are almost extended — do not lock the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to shoulder level, keeping the same neutral grip.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady tempo, stable bench contact, and neutral wrist alignment.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, dumbbells are pressed upward with a neutral (hammer) grip to emphasize the upper chest. The movement uses a controlled press and descent to work the pectorals with assistance from the delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Lever Incline Hammer Chest Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is fully supported on the incline pad and the handles start roughly at upper-chest / clavicle level when grasped.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is fully supported on the incline pad and the handles start roughly at upper-chest / clavicle level when grasped.
Select an appropriate weight and sit with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and your lower back gently pressed into the bench (avoid excessive arching).
Reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral (palms facing each other) 'hammer' grip; pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the chest.
Begin with elbows bent and positioned at about a 30–45° angle from the torso (not flared fully out), wrists neutral and aligned with forearms.
Exhale and press the handles upward and slightly together in a controlled motion until your arms are nearly extended — avoid locking the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top and consciously squeeze the upper pectorals while maintaining scapular stability.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly back to the start position, keeping control of the weight and maintaining the same elbow angle and shoulder position.
Complete desired reps and return the lever to the safety/rest position before releasing the handles; avoid using momentum or excessive shoulder elevation during the set.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline lever press machine using a neutral (hammer) grip, this exercise drives the handles upward to target the upper chest. It emphasizes the upper pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps for pressing and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Twist Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with feet planted flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with feet planted flat on the floor.
Pick up a pair of dumbbells and lie supine on the incline bench; hold the dumbbells at shoulder level with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and elbows bent about 45° from your torso.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly so your upper back stays in contact with the bench.
Exhale and press both dumbbells up and slightly together while rotating your wrists so that at the top your palms face forward and the dumbbells are parallel or lightly touching.
Pause briefly at the top and consciously squeeze the upper chest (pectorals).
Inhale and lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to the start position while rotating the wrists back to the neutral grip, keeping elbows under the wrists.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a controlled tempo, full scapular stability, and avoiding flaring the elbows excessively outward.
exercises.description: An incline dumbbell press with a twisting motion that emphasizes the upper chest while adding a rotational component to engage the shoulders and forearms. Performed lying supine on an incline bench, pressing the weights up and rotating the wrists so the palms face forward at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squeeze Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the low back.
Hold a pair of dumbbells together so their inner plates touch and palms face each other; position the dumbbells directly over your sternum with elbows bent.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the dumbbells squeezed together throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight up until your arms are nearly fully extended while maintaining the inward squeeze and keeping elbows at about a 30–45° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly at the top focusing on contracting the pectorals, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position until they touch your chest or sternum area.
Keep tension on the dumbbells (do not let them separate) and avoid flaring the elbows excessively; repeat for desired reps.
Use a controlled tempo and choose a weight that allows you to maintain the squeeze and proper form for each rep.
exercises.description: Supine dumbbell press where the two dumbbells are held tightly together (squeezed) throughout the movement to emphasize chest contraction. Press the weights up while maintaining the squeeze, then lower under control to maximize pectoral activation.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a flat bench inside the Smith machine and set safety stops at or just below chest level; load an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a flat bench inside the Smith machine and set safety stops at or just below chest level; load an appropriate weight.
Lie supine on the bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and knees bent, maintaining a neutral spine and slight retraction of the shoulder blades.
Grip the bar with a medium grip (slightly wider than shoulder width); wrists stacked over elbows and elbows angled about 45 degrees from the torso.
Unlatch the bar (rotate or lift depending on machine) and fully extend the arms to take the bar off the catches — keep the bar steady before lowering.
Inhale, then slowly lower the bar under control to mid-chest (nipple line) while keeping the elbows tracked and the chest engaged.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward powerfully until the arms are nearly extended (do not hyperextend the elbows).
Control the descent for the next rep; after your set, return the bar to the catches/safety pins before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Smith bench press is a supine barbell pressing movement performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes the pectorals through a fixed vertical/slightly angled bar path. It uses controlled lowering to the chest and pressing back up to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squeeze Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the low back.
Hold a pair of dumbbells together so their inner plates touch and palms face each other; position the dumbbells directly over your sternum with elbows bent.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the dumbbells squeezed together throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight up until your arms are nearly fully extended while maintaining the inward squeeze and keeping elbows at about a 30–45° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly at the top focusing on contracting the pectorals, then inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position until they touch your chest or sternum area.
Keep tension on the dumbbells (do not let them separate) and avoid flaring the elbows excessively; repeat for desired reps.
Use a controlled tempo and choose a weight that allows you to maintain the squeeze and proper form for each rep.
exercises.description: Supine dumbbell press where the two dumbbells are held tightly together (squeezed) throughout the movement to emphasize chest contraction. Press the weights up while maintaining the squeeze, then lower under control to maximize pectoral activation.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Neutral grip Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
Stand between the stacks in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) and take one handle in each hand with palms facing each other.
Step forward slightly until you feel tension in the cables, lean your torso forward a few degrees and keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to prevent trunk rotation or excessive arching.
Exhale and bring your hands together in a controlled horizontal arc in front of your chest, squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Hold the contracted position for 1–2 seconds, then inhale and slowly reverse the motion, letting the cables return your arms to the starting wide position while maintaining the elbow bend.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding locking the elbows or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly using a neutral (palms-facing) grip that isolates the chest by bringing the hands together in a horizontal arc. Performed from a staggered stance with a slight elbow bend to emphasize the pectoral squeeze while the cables provide continuous tension.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles/pads are level with the mid-chest; sit upright with your back flat against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your forearms against the pads with a slight bend in the elbows; start with arms out to the sides and shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Exhale and drive the handles forward and together in a smooth arc by contracting the pectorals, keeping the elbows slightly bent and wrists neutral.
Pause briefly when the hands/handles meet or are directly in front of your chest, feeling the squeeze in the pectorals.
Inhale and slowly allow the levers to return to the start position under control, maintaining shoulder blade stability and avoiding rapid letting go or trunk leaning.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; use a weight that allows full control of both concentric and eccentric phases without excessive shoulder or back movement.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly performed on a lever/pec-deck: you bring the arms together in front of the chest against resistance to isolate the pectoral muscles. The movement uses a slight elbow bend and controlled arc to emphasize the inner chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Smith Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
instructions: Set an incline bench inside the Smith machine at a 30–45° angle and load the bar with appropriate weight, securing plates with collars.
Sit on the bench and lie back so your head, upper back, and buttocks are fully supported (supine on an incline bench); plant feet flat on the floor for stability.
Position yourself so the bar aligns with the upper chest / clavicle area at the bottom of the movement; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades lightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away; keep wrists neutral and elbows angled about 30–45° from the torso.
Unrack the bar by rotating the bar hooks or lifting slightly and sliding it out of the safety catches, keeping the motion controlled.
Inhale and lower the bar in a slow, controlled motion to the upper chest (just below the clavicle), keeping the elbows under the bar and the forearms vertical.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back toward the rack until arms are nearly extended (do not fully lock the elbows).
Maintain a tight core and do not over-arch the lower back; keep the head and upper back in contact with the bench throughout the set.
After the final repetition, carefully return the bar to the safety hooks by rotating it back into the rack or guiding it onto the stops.
Use safety stops or a spotter as needed and choose a controlled tempo to prioritize form and avoid shoulder strain.
exercises.description: Incline Smith bench press performed on an incline bench in a Smith machine to emphasize the upper portion of the pectorals; the fixed bar path provides stability while pressing from the upper chest toward lockout. Great for building upper chest mass with controlled, guided movement.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Neutral grip Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about chest/shoulder height and attach neutral handles (or a short rope) to each side.
Stand between the stacks in a staggered stance (one foot forward for balance) and take one handle in each hand with palms facing each other.
Step forward slightly until you feel tension in the cables, lean your torso forward a few degrees and keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and brace your core to prevent trunk rotation or excessive arching.
Exhale and bring your hands together in a controlled horizontal arc in front of your chest, squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Hold the contracted position for 1–2 seconds, then inhale and slowly reverse the motion, letting the cables return your arms to the starting wide position while maintaining the elbow bend.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement slow and controlled and avoiding locking the elbows or using momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable fly using a neutral (palms-facing) grip that isolates the chest by bringing the hands together in a horizontal arc. Performed from a staggered stance with a slight elbow bend to emphasize the pectoral squeeze while the cables provide continuous tension.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered or shoulder-width stance with knees soft and core braced (slight forward lean at the hips).
Reach down and grab each handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip, palms facing in or up, elbows slightly bent and fixed in that position throughout the movement.
Begin with the arms out to the sides and low (handles near hips or knees) and inhale.
Exhale and pull the handles upward and inward in a wide arc, keeping the elbows slightly bent, bringing the hands together or in front of the chest/upper sternum.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze the pectorals while avoiding excessive shoulder shrugging.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, allowing the cables to return the arms to the start position while maintaining tension in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions and re-rack safely; keep torso stable and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly is a low-to-high cable chest fly that targets the pectorals by sweeping the arms upward and together from low pulley positions; performed standing with a slight forward lean and a soft bend in the elbows to emphasize the chest contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Lever Pec Deck Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are roughly at mid-chest level when seated and your back rests flat against the pad.
Select an appropriate weight and sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest up and shoulders down and back.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and position your upper arms roughly parallel to the floor with a small bend in the elbows.
Start with the handles out to the sides so your chest is stretched but shoulders are not overstretched; maintain a stable torso against the back pad.
Exhale and drive the handles together in a smooth horizontal arc by contracting the pectorals, bringing your hands close together or slightly in front of the chest.
Pause briefly at peak contraction, squeezing the chest while keeping elbows slightly bent and avoiding shoulder shrugging.
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement under control, allowing the arms to return to the start position until you feel a controlled stretch in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding leaning forward or using momentum to move the weight.
exercises.description: Seated machine chest fly that brings the arms together in a horizontal arc to isolate and contract the pectoral muscles. Performed on a lever/pec-deck machine with the chest supported and elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Twisted Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with feet flat on the floor.

Pick up a pair of dumbbells and lie back so your head, upper back and butt are supported (supine on an incline). Start with the dumbbells held above your upper chest, arms extended but with a slight bend in the elbows, palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable base; keep a soft bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, keeping the same elbow angle, until you feel a stretch across the upper chest but not beyond a comfortable range.

As you press the dumbbells back up, rotate (twist) your forearms so the palms turn slightly toward your face/each other and the dumbbells meet or pass just above the sternum — squeeze the pectorals at the top.
Exhale as you complete the concentric phase, maintaining control and avoiding banging the weights together; pause briefly at the top to maximize contraction.
Repeat for the desired reps, using a controlled tempo; avoid excessive range that causes shoulder pain and keep the movement driven by the chest, not the shoulders.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a dumbbell fly with a controlled arc and a forearm twist at the top to emphasize the upper chest. Keep a small bend in the elbows and squeeze the chest as the weights come together.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and select two dumbbells of appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench and plant your feet flat on the floor; lie back so your head, upper back and hips are supported (supine on an incline bench).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip; bring the dumbbells to shoulder level with elbows bent (~90°) and wrists stacked over elbows.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades slightly, and keep the elbows under the wrists (avoid excessive flaring).
Exhale and press both dumbbells up and slightly together until your arms are almost extended — do not lock the elbows at the top.
Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to shoulder level, keeping the same neutral grip.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady tempo, stable bench contact, and neutral wrist alignment.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, dumbbells are pressed upward with a neutral (hammer) grip to emphasize the upper chest. The movement uses a controlled press and descent to work the pectorals with assistance from the delts and triceps.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
instructions: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
Sit on the bench, then lie back so your head is lower than your hips (decline position) and hook your feet under the pads to prevent sliding.
Grip the bar with a slightly wider-than-shoulder-width overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and shoulder blades retracted and depressed.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar over your lower chest/upper sternum; have a spotter if lifting heavy.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to touch or come just above the lower chest/upper abdomen (lower sternum), keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle to the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until the arms are nearly straight (do not lock aggressively).
Maintain tightness through the chest and core, keep the scapulae retracted, and avoid excessive arching of the lower back throughout the set.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar safely with the help of a spotter when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell pressing movement performed on a decline bench that emphasizes the lower portion of the pectorals. Lying with the head lower than the hips, you lower the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen and press it back up.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Fly
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to a high or slightly above-shoulder position and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered (split) stance or feet shoulder-width apart with a slight forward lean from the hips so you have stable balance; brace your core.
Grasp a handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-down grip and take one step forward until you feel light tension in the cables; keep a small bend in the elbows throughout.
Start with your arms out to the sides roughly at shoulder height (or slightly higher for a high-cable fly) and chest lifted; retract your shoulder blades slightly to protect the joints.
Exhale and bring the handles together in a controlled arc in front of your chest at midline, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the finish.
Pause briefly with hands meeting or slightly overlapping, then inhale and slowly resist the cables as you return your arms to the start position using the same arcing path.
Keep the movement smooth and avoid locking the elbows, excessive torso rotation, or shrugging the shoulders; maintain core tension for stability.
exercises.description: Standing cable fly performed with the pulleys set high to mid-high engages the chest by bringing the arms together in a wide arc; the shoulders and core stabilize the movement. It emphasizes the pectoral fibers with controlled concentric adduction and slow eccentric return.

exercises.name: Cable Alternative Fly
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys at a low or mid position (roughly at or slightly below chest height when standing) and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys at a low or mid position (roughly at or slightly below chest height when standing) and attach single-handle grips.
Place a flat bench centered between the cable towers and lie supine (on your back) with feet flat on the floor and head supported by the bench.
Reach up and grab one handle in each hand with a neutral or palms-facing grip; start with the arms out to the sides and elbows slightly bent (soft elbows).
Retract and stabilize your shoulder blades on the bench to minimize shoulder elevation and protect the spine.
Exhale and bring one hand (or alternate hands) in a smooth arc toward the center of your chest, maintaining the same slight elbow bend and keeping the motion driven by the pectorals.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction with the hands meeting or slightly overlapping at midline, squeezing the chest.
Inhale and slowly return the working arm to the start position under control, resisting the cable's pull; keep torso and hips stable and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Repeat for the desired reps, alternating arms if performing the alternating variation; keep tempo controlled and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: A supine cable fly performed on a flat bench where each arm works alternately to bring the handle across the chest, emphasizing horizontal adduction of the shoulder to target the pectorals. The exercise uses a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to isolate the chest.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
