exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted).
instructions: Sit on the bench/seat facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent; select an appropriate weight.
Lean forward from the hips and reach to grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders rounded forward (scapulae protracted).
Keep a neutral spine and a slight chest-up posture; initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades (pull shoulders down and back) before bending the elbows.

Pull the handle toward your torso (near the lower chest/upper abdomen) driving the elbows back and keeping them close to your sides; squeeze the shoulder blades together at the end of the concentric phase.
Pause briefly at full contraction, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding excessive torso swing or leaning back.
Slowly extend the arms and allow the shoulder blades to protract as you return to the starting position with control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining neutral spine, controlled tempo, and full scapular retraction on each rep.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed from a seated position that primarily targets the latissimus dorsi while working the mid/upper back and arms. It develops scapular retraction and elbow flexion strength through a controlled pull and release.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

exercises.name: Cable Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment and select a light-to-moderate weight that allows strict form.

Stand facing the machine about 1–2 feet away, feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent; hinge forward at the hips so your torso is angled forward (standing bent-over position) with a neutral spine.
Reach up and grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) about shoulder-width apart; maintain a small, natural bend in the elbows throughout (do not lock).
Brace your core and depress your shoulder blades slightly (avoid shrugging) so tension is felt in the lats before starting the pull.

Exhale and pull the bar down in a controlled arc by extending the shoulders and pulling with the lats until the bar reaches your upper thighs or mid-thigh level; keep the arms long and the elbows fixed in their slight bend.
Squeeze the lats and hold the bottom position briefly while keeping the ribs down and spine neutral.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, resisting the cable so the lats remain under tension; do not let the lower back round as you return.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and long-arm position on every repetition.
exercises.description: Standing cable straight-arm pulldowns hinge from the hips and pull a high cable down with mostly straight arms to load the lats through a long-range extension. The movement emphasizes scapular depression and shoulder extension while keeping the torso braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

exercises.name: Lever One Arm Lateral High Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and chest/back pad so the handle is reachable with your arm extended overhead while seated; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and chest/back pad so the handle is reachable with your arm extended overhead while seated; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your chest braced against the pad, feet flat on the floor, core engaged and torso stable to prevent twisting.
Reach up and grasp the single high handle with one hand (neutral or pronated grip) while keeping the shoulder down and away from the ear.
Initiate the movement by driving the elbow down and back in a lateral arc, leading with the elbow so the hand moves toward the side of your ribs and the shoulder blade retracts and rotates downward.
Squeeze the upper back (scapulae) at the end range for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso still and avoiding excessive shoulder elevation or rotation.
Slowly return the handle to the start position with control, fully extending the arm while maintaining tension in the working muscles.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat. Maintain steady breathing (exhale while pulling, inhale while returning).
exercises.description: A single-arm high pulley/lever row performed from a seated position that pulls the handle laterally and upward to emphasize the upper back and rear shoulder musculature. It focuses on scapular retraction and elbow-led pulling to target the upper back and lats while the arm and grip work the biceps and forearms.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Shrug
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
instructions: Sit upright on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.

Pick up a barbell with a double overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width to slightly wider, arms fully extended so the bar hangs in front of your thighs; keep your torso neutral and head in a natural position.
Brace your core and maintain a tall, upright posture (no leaning forward or backward) throughout the movement.
Without bending the elbows, lift (shrug) the shoulders straight up toward the ears by elevating the scapulae; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds to maximize contraction of the traps.
Lower the shoulders slowly and under control to the starting position, allowing a full stretch of the traps between reps.
Breathe out as you shrug up and inhale as you lower; perform the desired number of reps while keeping movement strict and controlled.
exercises.description: A seated barbell shrug isolates the trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding a barbell; performing it seated reduces leg drive so the traps take most of the load. It emphasizes strong, vertical shoulder elevation (scapular elevation) with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Lateral Bent over
exercises.notes: Pull the handle down towards your side, keeping your arm straight, then slowly return to the starting position.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, bend at the hips, and grab the cable handle with one hand.
Pull the handle down towards your side, keeping your arm straight, then slowly return to the starting position.
Maintain a neutral spine and engage your core throughout the movement.
exercises.description: The Cable One Arm Lateral Bent Over exercise targets the lats while improving upper back strength and stability.

exercises.name: Cable Lying Extension Pullover (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a rope handle to the cable and set the pulley to a low or mid-low position in front of the bench.
instructions: Setup: Attach a rope handle to the cable and set the pulley to a low or mid-low position in front of the bench. Position the bench so your head is near the cable column.

Starting position: Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor for stability, knees bent if needed. Reach up and grab the rope with both hands (neutral grip), arms extended overhead but with a small bend in the elbows. Maintain a neutral spine.

Body position cues: Keep your ribs down and core braced to avoid excessive lower-back arching; shoulders should be relaxed away from the ears. Hands start above or slightly behind your head depending on pulley placement.

Pulling phase: Exhale and pull the rope in a controlled arc using your lats and shoulder extensors, bringing your hands down and back toward your hips/upper thighs. Keep the elbows slightly bent and fixed in that angle (no large elbow bending).

Peak contraction: Stop when your hands reach the upper-thigh/hip area or you feel a full lat contraction without forcing the shoulder into discomfort. Squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement.
Return phase: Inhale and slowly reverse the movement, allowing the rope to return overhead under control until you feel a stretch in the lats and chest. Do not let the weight slam the pulley.

Repetitions and safety: Use a weight that allows full range with strict form. Avoid shrugging the shoulders or using momentum; keep movement smooth and controlled. Stop or reduce range if you experience shoulder pain.
exercises.description: Lying on a flat bench with a rope attached to a cable, you extend the shoulders in an arc from an overhead position down toward the hips, emphasizing a lat-driven pullover motion. The rope attachment allows a greater range of motion and a strong contraction of the lats while the chest and triceps assist and the serratus stabilizes the shoulder girdle.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it securely on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it securely on the floor.
Select a pair of dumbbells and sit at the end of the bench, then carefully lie prone with your chest supported on the bench and feet on the floor or toes braced for stability.
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a neutral or palms-facing-in grip on the dumbbells; maintain a neutral neck and spine.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades back and down to initiate the movement.
Drive your elbows up and back, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs/waist while keeping elbows close to the body and squeezing the upper back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining scapular retraction and avoid shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Lower the dumbbells slowly under control to the starting position until the arms are nearly extended, keeping tension in the back throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the concentric (pull) and breathing in on the eccentric (lower).
Keep movements controlled, avoid torso rotation or using momentum, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain scapular control.
exercises.description: A prone row performed on an incline bench where the chest is supported and dumbbells are pulled toward the torso to emphasize the upper back. It uses a controlled scapular retraction and elbow drive to work the mid/upper back and pulling musculature.

exercises.name: Cable Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached to a low or mid pulley; feet shoulder-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
Grasp one or two handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) or overhand grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; keep a neutral neck and spine.
Slightly bend the knees and brace your core to stabilize the torso; do not lean forward or backward.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting the trapezius—do not roll or rotate the shoulders.
Hold the peak contraction briefly (about 1 second) while maintaining straight arms and a neutral neck.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the start in a controlled manner until traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement strict and avoiding momentum; reduce weight if you must use arm or torso movement to lift.
exercises.description: Standing cable shrugs target the trapezius by elevating the shoulders against resistance from a cable. Performed with arms straight at the sides, the movement emphasizes a vertical shrug with controlled lowering.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Lateral Bent over
exercises.notes: Pull the handle down towards your side, keeping your arm straight, then slowly return to the starting position.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, bend at the hips, and grab the cable handle with one hand.
Pull the handle down towards your side, keeping your arm straight, then slowly return to the starting position.
Maintain a neutral spine and engage your core throughout the movement.
exercises.description: The Cable One Arm Lateral Bent Over exercise targets the lats while improving upper back strength and stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Wide Pulldown (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the machine with an appropriate amount of weight and sit on the seat with your feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Load the machine with an appropriate amount of weight and sit on the seat with your feet flat on the floor.
Secure your thighs under the leg pads so your body remains stable; sit upright with a neutral spine and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Reach up and grasp the wide handles with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Start with your arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed but not shrugged.
Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving the handles toward your upper chest while retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your lats.
Maintain a slight backward lean at the torso (not excessive) and keep the chest up as you reach the bottom of the movement; avoid using momentum or swinging.
Pause briefly at the contraction while maintaining tension in the lats, then slowly and under control allow the handles to return to the start position with arms fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movements smooth and controlled and breathing out on the pull and inhaling on the return.
exercises.description: Seated wide-grip pulldown performed on a plate-loaded lever machine that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi and upper back by pulling the handles down with a wide, overhand grip. The movement uses elbow-driven shoulder extension and scapular retraction to load the lats while the arms and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Reverse Shrug
exercises.notes: Adjust a high pulley and attach a wide bar.
instructions: Adjust a high pulley and attach a wide bar. Sit on the bench facing the machine with knees braced under any pads and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the bar with a wide, overhand grip and fully extend your arms overhead; sit tall with a neutral spine and braced core (seated, upright).
Without bending the elbows, initiate the movement by depressing and retracting the scapulae—pull your shoulder blades down and together as far as comfortable.
Hold the contracted position for a brief second, keeping arms straight and avoiding elevation of the neck or shrugging forward.
Slowly and under control allow the shoulder blades to return to the start (up/out) position, maintaining tension in the traps and upper back.
Repeat for desired reps. Breathe out while depressing/retracting the scapulae and inhale as you return to the start.
exercises.description: A seated cable variation that emphasizes scapular depression and retraction to work the trapezius and upper back. With the pulley set high and the arms extended overhead, the movement pulls the shoulder blades down and together without bending the elbows.

exercises.name: Cable Squat Row (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set a low cable pulley and attach a rope.
instructions: Set a low cable pulley and attach a rope. Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart and the cable between your legs.
Grab the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip using both hands and take a couple steps back so the cable has tension; stand tall with chest up and core braced.

Initiate the movement by sitting back into a squat (knees tracking toes) until thighs are roughly parallel to parallel or slightly above, keeping weight through the heels and torso slightly leaned forward but chest lifted.

From the bottom of the squat, drive through the heels to stand up while simultaneously pulling the rope toward your torso: lead with the elbows, squeeze the shoulder blades together and flare the rope ends apart at the finish.
Fully extend the hips at the top while keeping the elbows tucked close to the body and shoulders down; pause briefly and emphasize a lat/upper-back contraction.
Slowly reverse the motion: extend the arms forward to release the row while controlling the descent back into the squat to the start position, maintaining core tension throughout.
Repeat for desired reps, maintaining coordinated timing (stand+row together, lower+reach together) and controlled tempo to avoid momentum.
exercises.description: A compound cable exercise that combines a squat with a horizontal row using a rope attachment to work the back (lats) while driving through the legs; it trains lower-body pushing and upper-body pulling in one coordinated movement.

exercises.name: Cable Front Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set the pulley to chest/low level and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the pulley to chest/low level and select an appropriate weight. Sit facing the machine on the bench with your chest against the vertical pad (chest-supported position).
Place your feet flat on the floor or footrest, knees slightly bent, and reach forward to grasp the single handle or bar with a neutral or overhand grip. Arms should start extended but not locked.
Maintain a neutral spine and braced core; keep your chest pressed gently into the pad so the torso remains stable throughout the movement.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows backward and retracting (squeezing) your shoulder blades together, pulling the handle toward the upper abdomen or lower chest.
Keep elbows close to your body and avoid excessive shrugging or leaning; drive the movement with the back muscles rather than the arms.
Hold the contracted position for 1 second, emphasizing the squeeze across the lats and upper back.
Slowly extend your arms and allow the shoulders to protract under control until you return to the start position, maintaining chest contact with the pad.
Breathe out during the pull (concentric) and inhale as you return (eccentric). Repeat for desired repetitions.
exercises.description: A seated cable row variation performed facing the machine with chest support that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi and mid-back by pulling the handle toward the torso while keeping the chest braced. Focus is on scapular retraction and controlled concentric and eccentric movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Grip Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a reverse (palms-up / supinated) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine, and keep a slight bend in the knees (standing, bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the dumbbells under your chest; brace your core and keep the neck in line with the spine.
Initiate the pull by driving the elbows upward and backward, pulling the dumbbells toward the lower ribcage/upper waist while keeping elbows close to the body.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the shoulder blades together without rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control until the arms are extended, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine throughout.
Breathe out as you pull the weights up and inhale as you lower them; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the lats while increasing biceps involvement. The lifter hip-hinges and pulls the dumbbells toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or pronated grip.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend), keeping the spine neutral and eyes looking a few feet ahead.
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with weights beneath you; brace your core to prevent rounding of the lower back.
Pull the dumbbells toward your lower ribcage or waist by driving your elbows up and back, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a tight upper back, then lower the weights slowly under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps while keeping the torso still—avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation.
Breathe out as you row the weights up and inhale as you lower them; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell row is a standing, hip-hinged pulling exercise that targets the muscles of the upper back while also engaging the lats and arms. It develops horizontal pulling strength and scapular retraction while the spine and forearms stabilize the position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a high pulley (above head height) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a high pulley (above head height) and select an appropriate weight.
Sit on a bench facing the machine with your chest pressed firmly against the vertical chest support pad and feet flat on the floor for stability.
Reach up and grab the handle with a neutral or overhand grip; allow the working arm to start extended overhead while keeping your torso braced against the pad.
Engage your scapula (pull the shoulder blade slightly down and back) before initiating the pull to protect the shoulder.

Pull the handle downward and back in a controlled arc so the elbow travels behind your torso and the hand finishes near the upper chest/side, squeezing the lats and upper back at the end of the concentric phase.
Pause briefly at the contracted position while maintaining chest contact with the pad and avoiding torso rotation or leaning.
Slowly return the handle to the start position with control, allowing the arm to extend fully while maintaining scapular control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Keep breathing: exhale while pulling, inhale while returning.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm high cable row performed with the chest braced against a vertical pad; pull the handle down and back to drive the elbow behind the torso and load the lats and upper back. This isolates one side for focused unilateral strength and scapular retraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Row (rack support)
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a slight bend in both knees; maintain a neutral spine and long neck throughout.
instructions: Stand with a staggered stance (one foot forward, one foot back) beside a rack; place the hand nearest the rack on the rail at about hip height for support.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor while keeping a slight bend in both knees; maintain a neutral spine and long neck throughout.
Let the working arm hang straight down holding a dumbbell with a neutral or overhand grip, shoulder relaxed.
Brace your core and pull the dumbbell upward in a vertical path, leading with the elbow and driving it toward your ribcage/hip; keep the elbow close to your body.
Squeeze the shoulder blade toward the spine at the top of the movement and pause briefly to maximize lat/upper-back contraction.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position under control until the arm is fully extended, maintaining torso stability and avoiding rotation.
Complete the desired reps on one side, then switch sides so the opposite hand supports on the rack and repeat. Keep breathing steadily (exhale on the pull, inhale on the return).
exercises.description: A single-arm bent-over row performed with one hand bracing on a rack for support; the exercise emphasizes a unilateral pull to develop the lats while also engaging the upper back and arm muscles. The support hand and staggered stance increase stability and allow focus on a full, controlled contraction of the pulling side.

exercises.name: Cable Lying Extension Pullover (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a rope handle to the cable and set the pulley to a low or mid-low position in front of the bench.
instructions: Setup: Attach a rope handle to the cable and set the pulley to a low or mid-low position in front of the bench. Position the bench so your head is near the cable column.

Starting position: Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor for stability, knees bent if needed. Reach up and grab the rope with both hands (neutral grip), arms extended overhead but with a small bend in the elbows. Maintain a neutral spine.

Body position cues: Keep your ribs down and core braced to avoid excessive lower-back arching; shoulders should be relaxed away from the ears. Hands start above or slightly behind your head depending on pulley placement.

Pulling phase: Exhale and pull the rope in a controlled arc using your lats and shoulder extensors, bringing your hands down and back toward your hips/upper thighs. Keep the elbows slightly bent and fixed in that angle (no large elbow bending).

Peak contraction: Stop when your hands reach the upper-thigh/hip area or you feel a full lat contraction without forcing the shoulder into discomfort. Squeeze the lats briefly at the bottom of the movement.
Return phase: Inhale and slowly reverse the movement, allowing the rope to return overhead under control until you feel a stretch in the lats and chest. Do not let the weight slam the pulley.

Repetitions and safety: Use a weight that allows full range with strict form. Avoid shrugging the shoulders or using momentum; keep movement smooth and controlled. Stop or reduce range if you experience shoulder pain.
exercises.description: Lying on a flat bench with a rope attached to a cable, you extend the shoulders in an arc from an overhead position down toward the hips, emphasizing a lat-driven pullover motion. The rope attachment allows a greater range of motion and a strong contraction of the lats while the chest and triceps assist and the serratus stabilizes the shoulder girdle.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and torso vertical (back neutral).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Y Raise

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and torso vertical (back neutral).
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and torso vertical (back neutral).
Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and palms facing forward; rest the bar at upper chest/clavicle level.
Brace your core and squeeze your shoulder blades down and slightly back to stabilize the spine; keep head neutral with eyes forward.
Inhale, then press the bar upward in a vertical path by extending your elbows and driving through the delts until arms are nearly locked out overhead.
As the bar passes your forehead, slightly tilt your head back to allow clearance, then return your head to neutral at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the bar roughly over mid-foot, shoulder blades stable and ribs down to avoid excessive lower-back arching.
Exhale and slowly lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest/clavicle, keeping elbows slightly in front of the body.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a tight core and steady breathing; avoid bouncing the bar off the chest or flaring the ribs.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead press performed from a seated, upright position pressing a barbell from the upper chest/clavicle to full overhead lockout. Primarily targets the shoulder delts while engaging triceps and upper traps for assistance and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or neutral and elbows slightly in front of the body.
Keep the spine neutral and ribcage down to avoid hyperextending the lower back; squeeze the abs throughout.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders stacked over hips and the head neutral (you may slightly move your head back to allow the dumbbells to pass), keeping shoulder blades stable.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive torso lean or lower-back arching; if needed, use a lighter weight to maintain good form.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press is a compound shoulder exercise where you press dumbbells from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, primarily working the delts while engaging the triceps and upper back for support and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip.
Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep the shoulder blades relaxed but ready to retract.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc (horizontal abduction) leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and at shoulder height.
Squeeze the rear delts and pinch the shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, avoiding momentum or torso swing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement controlled and the torso stable throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell fly that isolates the posterior (rear) deltoids by horizontally abducting the arms from a hinged position. Performed with a hip hinge and slight elbow bend, the movement emphasizes the rear delts while engaging the upper back and traps to stabilize and retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Y Raise
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward (~30–45°), keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a flat, braced spine (standing bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a light dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is angled forward (~30–45°), keep a slight bend in the knees, and maintain a flat, braced spine (standing bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with the weights close to your thighs and shoulders relaxed away from your ears.
Keeping a small bend in the elbows and thumbs pointing up, raise the dumbbells up and out at about a 45° angle from the body to form a Y shape (lead with the thumbs).
Focus on scapular control—initiate the movement from the delts/upper back, squeeze the shoulder blades gently at the top without overarching the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for desired reps, using light weight to maintain strict form and avoid momentum or excessive neck/upper-trap shrugging.
exercises.description: Standing bent-over Y raise is a hip-hinged shoulder exercise that targets the delts by lifting light dumbbells in a Y-shape overhead while stabilizing the scapula. It emphasizes posterior/upper shoulder and upper back activation for improved posture and scapular control.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward (or slightly rotated) and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees, wrists stacked over elbows.
Inhale, then exhale as you press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly extended overhead; avoid fully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulders down and chest lifted (do not shrug excessively).
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Keep the back pressed against the bench and avoid excessive lower-back arching or leaning; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a back-supported bench to drive the weights overhead, primarily working the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with triceps and upper traps assisting. The movement emphasizes vertical pressing strength and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand (pronated) grip, arms extended down in front of you and weights close to your thighs.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body and leading with the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells until your elbows reach roughly chest/shoulder height or just below chin level, keeping wrists relatively neutral and palms facing your body or slightly inward.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but avoid excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms without locking the elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining an upright standing position, steady breathing, and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell upright row where you pull the weights vertically toward your upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with assistance from the trapezius, biceps, and forearms. Perform with a controlled motion and elbows leading to keep tension on the delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Cable Front Raise
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar or single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing the cable machine with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing upright).
Step 3: Grasp the bar with an overhand grip (palms down) about shoulder-width, arms extended down in front with a slight bend at the elbows.
Step 4: Maintain a neutral spine and keep shoulders back and down; avoid leaning or using momentum.
Step 5: Exhale and raise the bar straight forward and up with the arms (leading with the elbows) until your hands reach about shoulder height.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the top while keeping tension on the cable and the core engaged.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, keeping the slight elbow bend throughout.
Step 8: Repeat for the desired reps; avoid swinging the torso or using excessive weight to maintain strict delt activation.
exercises.description: A standing cable front raise performed from a low pulley that lifts the arms straight in front of the body to shoulder height, primarily working the front portion of the shoulder. It uses a controlled movement with a slight elbow bend to isolate the anterior delts.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest/shoulder level and select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your chest pressed firmly against the chest pad, feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your arms extended forward and a slight bend in the elbows, shoulders relaxed.
Exhale and pull the handles outward and back in a wide arc at shoulder height, leading with the elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Stop when your arms are roughly in line with your torso or you feel a strong contraction in the rear delts and upper back; keep movement controlled and avoid shrugging.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend and chest against the pad.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping the torso stationary and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated reverse fly performed on a lever machine to strengthen the posterior delts and upper back by horizontally abducting the arms while seated with chest supported. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and rear shoulder activation.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Face Pull
exercises.notes: Set a cable pulley to about eye- to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to about eye- to upper-chest height and attach a rope handle.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart or in a slight staggered stance, knees soft and spine neutral.
Grasp the rope with both hands using a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing inward) and take a step back so there is tension on the cable.
Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and pulling the rope toward your face, driving the elbows out to the sides so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
As you reach the end range, externally rotate the shoulders so the hands split apart and the rope ends are near your temples or ears; pause and squeeze the rear delts and upper back.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, extending the arms forward while maintaining scapular control until the hands return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping torso stable (no excessive leaning or momentum) and focusing on quality of movement and external rotation.
exercises.description: A standing cable face pull with a rope attachment that emphasizes the posterior delts and upper back by pulling the rope toward the face while externally rotating the shoulders. It develops rear shoulder strength, scapular retraction, and shoulder external rotators.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Shoulders Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft.
Place the barbell in a front-rack position resting on the upper chest/clavicle area with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and full grip around the bar.
Retract the shoulder blades slightly, keep the chest up and core braced (neutral spine); avoid excessive lumbar arching.
Take a full breath and begin the press by driving the bar upward in a straight line, slightly moving the head back as the bar passes and then returning the head forward under the bar.
Fully extend the elbows at the top with the bar directly over or slightly behind the head, shoulders stacked over hips and feet for balance.
Lower the bar under control back to the front-rack position to complete one rep while inhaling, keeping the elbows tracking slightly forward.
Repeat for desired repetitions, maintaining tight core engagement and controlled tempo; stop or reduce weight if you must excessively lean back to finish reps.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the delts while requiring the triceps and upper back to assist and stabilize. The lifter presses the barbell from shoulder level to full arm extension overhead while maintaining a braced core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with elbows bent ~90°, palms facing forward or slightly inward and wrists neutral.
Inhale, keep the spine neutral and shoulders down away from the ears.
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled motion until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead (avoid locking the elbows).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and keeping the torso stable.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Use a lighter weight or a spotter if form breaks down or if pressing heavy loads.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press is a compound pressing movement performed from a seated, back‑supported position that primarily develops the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise Wrist Straps with Back Support
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly soft (not locked).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly neutral and elbows roughly under the wrists.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a neutral spine and prevent lower-back arching.
Exhale and press both dumbbells straight overhead in a controlled path until your arms are nearly fully extended (avoid jamming the elbows).
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the chin tucked slightly; do not overextend the neck or lower back at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts and scapular stabilizers.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining core tension and full control on both ascent and descent.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell overhead press that primarily works the delts by pressing weights from shoulder height to full arm extension overhead, while the triceps and upper-back muscles stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.

Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.

Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
