The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise Wrist Straps with Back Support
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single handles. Stand centered between the two towers and grasp each handle with the opposite hand so the cables cross in front of you (or hold same-side handles if preferred).
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (neutral spine). Keep a slight bend in the elbows and wrists neutral.
Start with the handles low by your thighs; palms can face inward or slightly forward. Maintain a small forward lean from the hips if comfortable to allow a natural arc.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a smooth arc until your hands reach approximately shoulder height. Lead the movement with the elbow, not the wrist.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulders upward toward your ears.
Inhale and lower the handles slowly and under control back to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables throughout the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain strict form or if you begin using torso momentum.
exercises.description: A standing cable lateral raise performed from low pulleys to isolate the lateral head of the delts; cables provide constant tension while you lift the arms out to the sides to shoulder height. The movement is done with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo to minimize momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Front Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (palms facing your thighs).

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the middle of a flat bench (no back support) with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your front shoulders/clavicle in a front-rack position (hands just outside shoulder width, elbows slightly forward).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise Wrist Straps with Back Support
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Front Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (palms facing your thighs).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip‑width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides (palms facing your thighs).
Keep a slight bend in both elbows, shoulders down and back, chest up, and brace your abs to stabilize the torso (neutral spine).
Slowly raise one dumbbell straight in front of you (no swinging or shoulder rotation) until your arm is parallel to the floor and level with the shoulder; exhale as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top, keeping the wrist neutral and the elbow slightly bent, then inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position under control.
Immediately perform the same motion with the opposite arm, alternating sides each repetition while maintaining an upright torso and minimal movement from the hips.
Use a weight you can control for the full range without rocking or using momentum; reduce weight if you find yourself leaning back or swinging.
exercises.description: Standing single‑arm front raises with dumbbells that alternately lift each arm to shoulder height to target the front of the shoulder. Performed slowly and controlled to minimize momentum and maximize deltoid activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the middle of a flat bench (no back support) with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
instructions: Sit upright near the middle of a flat bench (no back support) with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width so your wrists are stacked over your elbows; rest the bar on your upper chest/clavicle area.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to create a stable upper-back platform; keep a neutral spine and a small natural lumbar curve (avoid excessive arching).
Unrack the bar (or lift it off the rack) and hold it just above the clavicle with elbows slightly forward of the bar and forearms vertical.

Exhale and press the bar straight up in a controlled line over the midline of your head until your arms are fully extended but not aggressively hyperextended; the bar should finish roughly over your shoulders/ears.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged, then inhale and lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest/clavicle.
Repeat for desired reps while maintaining a tight core, flat feet, and minimal torso lean; stop or rack the bar safely if form breaks or you cannot control the descent.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead press performed from a bench to drive the bar straight overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back for stabilization. The movement emphasizes a vertical press with a braced core to limit excessive spine extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press (without rack)
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your front shoulders/clavicle in a front-rack position (hands just outside shoulder width, elbows slightly forward).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your front shoulders/clavicle in a front-rack position (hands just outside shoulder width, elbows slightly forward).
Grip the bar with a firm overhand grip, wrists stacked under the bar and chest tall; take a full breath and brace your core and glutes to prevent lower-back arching.
Tighten your upper back and scapulae (retract slightly) and press your head back just enough to allow the bar to travel in a straight line.

Drive the barbell upward forcefully by extending the elbows and pressing through the shoulders until the arms are fully extended overhead; finish with the bar roughly over the midline of the body and the head moved slightly forward under the bar.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged and core braced, then inhale and lower the bar under control back to the starting front-rack position at the clavicle.
Perform repetitions with a steady tempo, keeping the torso rigid and avoiding excessive lumbar extension or forward collapse of the elbows.

If starting from the floor (no rack), use a controlled clean or push-press to bring the bar to the front-rack before beginning strict presses; lower weight to the floor only after finishing the set or rerack safely.
exercises.description: A standing barbell overhead press performed from the front-rack position that primarily develops the shoulder (delts) musculature while requiring upper-body pressing strength and core stability. Performed without a rack, the bar is pressed vertically from the shoulders to full arm extension overhead.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down at your sides and palms facing inward; keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and neck neutral, and avoid arching your lower back.
Exhale and lift both dumbbells upward and slightly forward in a controlled arc until your hands reach about shoulder height (arms roughly parallel to the floor).
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the delts and avoiding momentum or swinging.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting hanging position under control.
Perform the desired repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding use of torso momentum; reduce weight if you need to swing to complete reps.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench, this variation of the dumbbell raise emphasizes the front portion of the shoulder (anterior delts) by lifting the weights from a hanging position up to about shoulder height. The incline angle shifts some load onto the chest (pectorals) while isolating the delts and minimizing torso swing.

exercises.name: Cable Incline Y Raise Wrist Straps with Back Support
exercises.notes: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
instructions: Set a bench to an incline (about 30–45°) and position it so your back is fully supported against the pad.
Attach wrist straps to low pulley handles (both sides) and set a light–moderate weight so you can control the entire range.
Sit with feet flat on the floor, chest against the bench, and grasp the wrist straps so your arms hang down in front of you with a slight bend in the elbows; palms should face each other.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to stabilize the scapulae and maintain a neutral spine throughout the set.
Exhale and raise your arms up and out in a diagonal plane (about 30–45° forward of the frontal plane) to form a Y with your body; lead with the thumbs and keep elbows soft.
Lift until your arms are roughly in line with your head/ears (or you feel a solid contraction in the delts/traps), squeezing the upper back at the top.
Inhale and lower the arms slowly and under control back to the start position, resisting the cable’s pull.

Perform the desired reps while avoiding momentum, excessive lumbar extension, or letting the shoulders excessively shrug; keep the back supported by the bench and use wrist straps to minimize grip fatigue.
exercises.description: Seated on an incline bench with back support, you use low-set cables and wrist straps to lift the arms into a Y shape overhead, emphasizing the shoulder delts and the upper back/trap region. The movement is performed with a controlled lift and slow lowering to isolate the posterior/lateral delts and scapular stabilizers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Neutral Grip Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single-handle on a low cable pulley (near the floor) and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single-handle on a low cable pulley (near the floor) and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright facing forward with feet hip-width apart and take a small step away from the machine so there is tension on the cable; hold the handle in one hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip at your side.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and a neutral wrist; brace your core and maintain an upright torso (avoid leaning or rotating).
Exhale and raise the arm straight forward in a controlled motion to about shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor), leading with the elbow but keeping the hand in line with the forearm.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulder down (do not shrug), then inhale and lower the arm slowly back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides. Focus on using the deltoid to lift rather than swinging the body or using momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable front raise performed with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip to isolate the anterior deltoid while the cable provides constant tension. Performed standing, it emphasizes controlled shoulder flexion from the front of the body.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Lying Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so you can lie face down (prone) with your chest supported and feet braced on the floor or toes on the floor behind you.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so you can lie face down (prone) with your chest supported and feet braced on the floor or toes on the floor behind you.

Grasp a barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly wider) grip, palms facing down (pronated) or neutral if using a specialty bar, letting the bar hang straight down toward the floor with a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and keep your neck neutral, chest pressed into the bench to prevent using torso momentum.

Exhale and raise the barbell out to your sides in a controlled arc by horizontally abducting the shoulders, leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or at shoulder level.
Pause briefly at the top and focus on squeezing the rear delts, traps, and upper back; avoid shrugging the shoulders or using excessive momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position under control, maintaining the slight elbow bend and chest contact with the bench.
Perform the desired number of reps with smooth tempo; choose light-to-moderate weight to maintain strict form and protect the shoulder joint.
exercises.description: Prone on an incline bench, lift a barbell out to the sides in a horizontal abduction, emphasizing contraction of the rear delts. The chest is supported on the bench while the arms move in an arc to shoulder level with a slight elbow bend.

exercises.name: Dumbbell One Arm Shoulder Press (VERSION 2)
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft; hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the palm facing forward or slightly inward.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine; place the free hand on your hip or at your side for balance.
Exhale and press the dumbbell straight upward by extending the elbow and lifting the arm until it is fully or nearly fully overhead without overarching the lower back.
Keep the shoulder blade stable and avoid shrugging the working shoulder; do not lean or rotate the torso toward the working side.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell under control back to the starting shoulder-height position.
Perform the desired number of reps on one side, then switch arms and repeat. Use a lighter weight if you cannot maintain good posture or if the lower back arches.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm dumbbell shoulder press performed from shoulder height to overhead to target the delts. The movement uses the triceps and serratus anterior for elbow extension and scapular stability while the core resists trunk tilt.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press (parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back fully supported, feet flat on the floor and core braced (seated, back-supported).
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back fully supported, feet flat on the floor and core braced (seated, back-supported).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with a parallel grip (palms facing each other), elbows roughly under wrists and pointing slightly forward.
Keep the chest up and shoulder blades stable; exhale and press the dumbbells straight up until the arms are nearly extended but not locked.
Pause briefly at the top with wrists neutral and head in a neutral position, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding excessive lower back arching or flaring the elbows too wide.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press with a parallel (palms-facing) grip; presses dumbbells from shoulder height overhead to work the delts while the triceps and upper back stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
instructions: Stand feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keep a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (bent-over position).
Let your arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain a braced core and avoid rounding the back.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the ground (level with the shoulders).
Squeeze the rear delts and shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause, avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start under control, resisting any momentum; repeat for desired reps.
Keep the movement strict: use lighter weight if you must swing the torso or bend the elbows excessively, and breathe out on the lift and in on the lower.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell raise that isolates the rear (posterior) deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides while the torso is hinged forward. It emphasizes shoulder horizontal abduction and scapular retraction with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Front Raise
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.

Stand upright (neutral spine) facing away from the machine with feet hip-width apart or in a slight staggered stance for stability; grasp the handle with the working hand using an overhand grip (palm facing down) and let the arm hang in front of your thigh with a small bend in the elbow.
Brace your abs, keep your chest tall and shoulders down, and hold the non-working hand on your hip or at your side to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and raise the working arm straight forward in a controlled arc until the hand reaches shoulder height (arm roughly parallel to the floor); keep the elbow slightly bent and the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the delts; avoid shrugging the shoulder or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the arm back to the starting position under control, resisting the cable.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch to the other arm. Maintain strict form—no swinging or using body momentum.
exercises.description: A unilateral cable front raise that isolates the anterior deltoid by lifting a single arm straight in front of the body from a low pulley. It provides constant tension through the range of motion and requires core stabilization to prevent torso movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Neutral Grip Front Raise
exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent at about 90 degrees and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Close grip Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and shoulder blades retracted; position the bar on the rack directly above your eyes.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral/hammer grip (palms facing each other, thumbs pointing up) and let your arms hang fully extended to establish the start position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent at about 90 degrees and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor, knees bent at about 90 degrees and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and upper arms resting close to your torso.
Keep your back straight and shoulders down; avoid leaning back or using body momentum.
Exhale and curl both dumbbells upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and elbows tucked to your sides.
Continue the curl until the dumbbells reach shoulder level and the biceps are fully contracted; squeeze briefly at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled motion, fully extending the elbows but maintaining tension in the muscles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, using controlled tempo and avoiding swinging or shifting the torso.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell curls are an isolation exercise that primarily target the biceps by flexing the elbow from a seated position. Sitting stabilizes the torso so the arms do the majority of the work, with the forearms assisting and the abs providing postural support.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Close grip Press
exercises.notes: Set up: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and shoulder blades retracted; position the bar on the rack directly above your eyes.
instructions: Set up: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and shoulder blades retracted; position the bar on the rack directly above your eyes.
Grip the bar with a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches between hands) with palms facing up and wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving it over your chest; keep your shoulder blades pinched and a slight arch in the upper back.
Descend: Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-to-lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked toward your torso (about a 30–45° angle) to target the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight, maintaining control and avoiding aggressive hyperextension of the elbows.
Repeat for the desired reps with a steady tempo (e.g., 2 seconds down, brief pause, 1 second up).
Safety: Use a spotter or set safety pins in the rack at chest height; keep wrists neutral and avoid flaring the elbows out wide to reduce shoulder strain.
exercises.description: A flat bench press variation performed with a narrow grip to emphasize the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and front delts. Performed lying supine on a bench, the bar is lowered to the mid/lower chest and pressed back up with elbows kept close to the body.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
Lean forward slightly at the hips and place the back of your upper arm (triceps area) or elbow against the inside of the same-side thigh so the elbow is fixed.
Brace your core and keep your upper arm stationary; this is a seated, supported position to isolate the biceps.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the dumbbell approaches shoulder level, focusing on contracting the biceps and keeping the wrist neutral.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top for 1 second without shrugging the shoulder or rocking the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the fully extended starting position with control (eccentric phase).
Avoid using body momentum or moving the upper arm; switch sides after completing the set for the opposite arm.
exercises.description: Seated, single-arm dumbbell curl performed with the working elbow braced against the inside of the same-side thigh to isolate the biceps. The exercise emphasizes a strict concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering to maximize biceps contraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral/hammer grip (palms facing each other, thumbs pointing up) and let your arms hang fully extended to establish the start position.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and feet flat on the floor; rest the back of your upper arms on the angled pad so elbows are slightly forward of the bench edge.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral/hammer grip (palms facing each other, thumbs pointing up) and let your arms hang fully extended to establish the start position.
Brace your core, keep shoulders down and retracted, and ensure the upper arms remain pressed to the pad throughout the movement.

Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing only at the elbows, driving the forearms upward while maintaining the neutral grip; lift until the dumbbells approach shoulder level or you reach a full comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the forearm muscles, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner back to full extension without lifting the upper arms off the pad.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding body swing, shoulder involvement, or wrist bending; choose a weight that allows strict form.
exercises.description: Seated preacher-style hammer curl using dumbbells with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip to isolate the forearms (brachioradialis) while limiting shoulder movement. The angled pad supports the upper arms so the elbows stay fixed and the movement comes from elbow flexion.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (weight-bearing, standing position) with feet hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ curl bar with an underhand/angled grip (hands on the inner grips, about shoulder width).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell resting against your thighs.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (weight-bearing, standing position) with feet hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ curl bar with an underhand/angled grip (hands on the inner grips, about shoulder width).
instructions: Stand upright (weight-bearing, standing position) with feet hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ curl bar with an underhand/angled grip (hands on the inner grips, about shoulder width).
Keep your chest up, shoulders back and down, core braced (engage abs) and elbows pinned to your sides; wrists should follow the bar's natural angle.
Exhale and curl the bar by flexing your elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and driving the forearms toward the shoulders until the biceps are fully contracted.
Pause for a brief 1 second at the top and squeeze the biceps, making sure not to use momentum or swing the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until arms are almost fully extended but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain strict form—avoid leaning back or letting the elbows drift forward to ensure biceps remain the primary movers.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ curl bar to isolate elbow flexion and emphasize the biceps while the angled bar reduces wrist strain. Performed with a supinated/angled grip and controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
Lean forward slightly at the hips and place the back of your upper arm (triceps area) or elbow against the inside of the same-side thigh so the elbow is fixed.
Brace your core and keep your upper arm stationary; this is a seated, supported position to isolate the biceps.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the dumbbell approaches shoulder level, focusing on contracting the biceps and keeping the wrist neutral.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top for 1 second without shrugging the shoulder or rocking the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the fully extended starting position with control (eccentric phase).
Avoid using body momentum or moving the upper arm; switch sides after completing the set for the opposite arm.
exercises.description: Seated, single-arm dumbbell curl performed with the working elbow braced against the inside of the same-side thigh to isolate the biceps. The exercise emphasizes a strict concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering to maximize biceps contraction.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell resting against your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell resting against your thighs.
Grip the bar with an overhand (pronated) grip, hands shoulder-width or slightly narrower, arms fully extended and elbows close to your sides.
Brace your core and keep your chest up and shoulders back to prevent torso momentum.

Exhale and curl the bar upward by bending at the elbows, keeping the wrists pronated and elbows stationary; lead with the forearms until the bar reaches about chest/upper-abdomen level or full elbow flexion.
Hold and squeeze briefly at the top, focusing on contracting the forearms and biceps without swinging the body.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control until arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using body momentum or letting the wrists rotate into a supinated grip.
exercises.description: Standing barbell reverse curl performed with an overhand (pronated) grip to emphasize the forearm extensor and brachioradialis muscles while also recruiting the biceps. Lift the bar by flexing the elbows while keeping the wrists pronated and the torso upright.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Wide grip Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (not prone or kneeling) with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (not prone or kneeling) with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Grip the barbell with a supinated (palms-up) wide grip — hands placed wider than shoulder width (about 1.25–1.5x shoulder width).
Let the bar hang at arm's length in front of your thighs with wrists neutral and elbows close to your sides (avoid letting elbows drift forward).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary so the movement comes from the biceps.
Raise the bar until your forearms are near or touching your biceps and hold a brief squeeze at the top (do not hyperflex the elbows).
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full arm extension — resist letting the weight drop or using torso momentum.
Keep the shoulders down and chest up throughout; avoid swinging the torso or using excessive back extension.
Perform repetitions with a controlled tempo (e.g., 1–2 seconds concentric, 2–3 seconds eccentric) and choose a weight that allows strict form.
exercises.description: Standing wide-grip barbell curl is a supinated (underhand) curl with hands placed wider than shoulder width that emphasizes the biceps while using the forearms and delts for stabilization. The bar is curled from thigh level up toward the shoulders with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Seated High Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Set two high pulleys to the top position and attach single handles (or a rope).
instructions: Set two high pulleys to the top position and attach single handles (or a rope). Select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright on a bench placed between the two pulleys with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and core braced (seated position).

Reach up and grab each handle with a neutral or palms-facing-in grip; cross the cables if preferred so the hands start slightly in front of the head with elbows bent and pointing up/forward (overhead starting position).
Keep upper arms stationary and close to the head; inhale and brace your core to prevent arching of the lower back.
Exhale and extend both elbows by driving the handles downward and slightly forward until the arms are fully straight but not hyperextended, focusing on squeezing the triceps.
Pause briefly at full extension, then slowly inhale and control the cables back to the starting position by allowing the forearms to flex at the elbow while keeping the upper arms fixed.
Perform all reps with controlled tempo, avoid letting the shoulders hike or the torso lean excessively, and maintain neutral wrists throughout the movement.
If needed, reduce the weight to maintain strict form and prevent momentum; keep breathing steady and the core engaged for spinal support.
exercises.description: Seated cable high triceps extension is an overhead triceps pressing movement performed from a seated position with the pulleys set high; it emphasizes elbow extension to target the triceps (particularly the long head) while the shoulders and core stabilize the torso. The movement uses cables/handles brought from above and extended downward until the arms are straight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell 21s
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides with a supinated (palms-up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and a dumbbell in each hand held at your sides with a supinated (palms-up) grip.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and elbows fixed at your sides throughout the movement — do not let the elbows travel forward.

Perform the first 7 reps (lower-half): from full arm extension curl the dumbbells only from the bottom to the halfway point (approximately to 90 degrees at the elbow), then lower back to full extension.
Immediately perform the next 7 reps (upper-half): curl the dumbbells from the halfway point up to full contraction at the shoulders, then lower back to the halfway point.
Immediately perform the final 7 reps (full-range): curl the dumbbells from full extension to full contraction and lower back to full extension for each rep.
Use controlled tempo (1–2 seconds concentric, 1–2 seconds eccentric), avoid swinging the torso or using momentum, and breathe out on the curl and in on the lower.
After completing all 21 reps, rest 60–90 seconds (or as programmed) before repeating. If fatigue causes form breakdown, reduce weight.
exercises.description: Dumbbell 21s is a standing biceps curl variation that uses 21 total reps divided into three segments (7 lower-half, 7 upper-half, 7 full-range) to increase time under tension and promote biceps hypertrophy and endurance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternate Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Curl one dumbbell up towards the shoulder while keeping the upper arm stationary, then lower it back down.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with dumbbells in each hand, palms facing forward.
Curl one dumbbell up towards the shoulder while keeping the upper arm stationary, then lower it back down.
Alternate between each arm, ensuring controlled movements throughout the exercise.
exercises.description: The Dumbbell Incline Alternate Biceps Curl targets the biceps while providing a stretch to the upper back. It is designed to isolate and strengthen the biceps muscles.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Seated Curls
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees at about 90 degrees, maintaining a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and knees at about 90 degrees, maintaining a neutral spine.
Pick up an EZ bar with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated or semi-supinated) grip on the angled sections so wrists are comfortable and forearms are engaged.
Start with your arms fully extended downward and the bar close to your thighs, elbows tucked close to your sides (not flared forward).
Exhale and curl the bar up by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and only moving the forearms; lift until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is near shoulder level.
Pause briefly at the top and actively squeeze the biceps; avoid rotating the shoulders or leaning back to gain momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with controlled tempo until arms are fully extended but elbows are not forcefully hyperextended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining strict form—no swinging or using the lower back—and keep wrists neutral throughout.
exercises.description: Seated EZ bar curls are a concentration-style arm exercise performed seated to minimize body swing while curling an angled (EZ) bar, emphasizing the biceps through a supinated/semi-supinated grip. The angled grip also recruits the forearms for grip and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Preacher Curl (Turned Torso)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Select a dumbbell of appropriate weight and sit on a preacher bench; rotate your torso toward the working arm so the inside of your upper arm rests flat and comfortably on the angled pad.
instructions: Step 1: Select a dumbbell of appropriate weight and sit on a preacher bench; rotate your torso toward the working arm so the inside of your upper arm rests flat and comfortably on the angled pad.
Step 2: Plant your feet firmly on the floor, keep your chest close to the pad, and brace with your non-working hand if needed for stability.
Step 3: Grasp the dumbbell with a supinated (palm-up) grip, letting your working arm hang down with a slight bend in the elbow — this is the start position.
Step 4: Exhale and curl the dumbbell upward by contracting the biceps, keeping the upper arm pressed to the pad and avoiding any shoulder or torso swing; rotate only as needed from the elbow.
Step 5: Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement with the forearm vertical (or slightly turned as needed for comfort), keeping the wrist neutral and stable.
Step 6: Inhale and lower the dumbbell slowly and under control back to the start position until the elbow is nearly extended, maintaining constant tension on the biceps.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps, then switch sides; avoid using momentum, letting the pad support the upper arm to isolate the biceps.
exercises.description: A single-arm preacher-style dumbbell curl performed with the torso rotated toward the working arm to better isolate the biceps. The upper arm is supported on the preacher pad so the elbow stays fixed while the forearm curls the weight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

exercises.name: Cable Seated One Arm Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the end of a flat bench with legs spread, feet flat on the floor and the pulley directly in front or slightly to the side of the working arm.
Reach down and grasp the handle with a supinated (palm-up) grip using one hand.
Rest the working elbow firmly against the inside of the same-side thigh near the knee; lean slightly forward from the hips so the upper arm is stable and vertical (seated, braced position).
Keep your torso still and core engaged; this prevents using body momentum to lift the weight.
Curl the handle upward by contracting the biceps, bringing the forearm toward the shoulder while keeping the upper arm stationary on the thigh.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly lower the handle back to the start position under control until the elbow is near full extension.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.
exercises.description: Seated one-arm concentration curl performed with a low cable to isolate the biceps by bracing the elbow against the inner thigh and curling the handle toward the shoulder. The cable provides constant tension through the movement for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
instructions: Set up a preacher bench and load a straight or EZ bar with an appropriate weight; sit so your chest contacts the pad and your armpits rest at the top edge of the pad.
Place feet flat on the floor and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip so your upper arms lie flat against the pad and wrists are neutral.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above full extension (do not lock the elbows); take a full breath in to brace the torso.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder or torso movement.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a strong biceps contraction (bar near chest/shoulder level), then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with controlled tension.
Keep the movement strict—no swinging, jerking, or lifting the elbows off the pad—and stop short of joint hyperextension at the bottom.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, adjusting grip width to slightly vary biceps emphasis as needed.
exercises.description: Seated on a preacher (incline) bench with upper arms supported on the pad, curl a barbell upward by flexing the elbows to isolate the biceps, then lower under control to stretch the muscle.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; grip the SZ bar with an underhand (palms up) reverse grip roughly shoulder-width apart.
Tuck your elbows close to your sides and keep your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and extend your elbows to push the bar down until your arms are nearly straight and triceps are fully contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid flaring the elbows.
Pause briefly at the bottom to feel the triceps contraction without locking the elbows or using torso momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, returning to about a 90-degree elbow angle or slightly less while maintaining elbow position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement tempo controlled and avoiding shoulder or torso involvement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip to emphasize elbow extension and load the triceps. Perform controlled extensions and returns while keeping the upper arms stationary to isolate the triceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Drag Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a barbell with a shoulder-width, supinated (palms-up) grip and arms fully extended in front of your thighs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Double Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down between your legs and palms facing up (supinated grip).

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Lean forward slightly and rest the working elbow on the inside of the same-side thigh just above the knee; let the arm hang fully extended (upright, seated position).

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Drag Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a barbell with a shoulder-width, supinated (palms-up) grip and arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; hold a barbell with a shoulder-width, supinated (palms-up) grip and arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back and down, and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Begin the rep by driving your elbows backward (toward your hips) while simultaneously pulling the bar straight up the front of your body so the bar stays in contact with or very close to your torso.
Focus on leading the movement with the elbows rather than swinging the wrists; the forearms should move upward as the elbows travel back so the bar drags along the body.
Continue until your forearms are vertical or the bar reaches chest/upper-abdomen level and you feel a full biceps contraction; briefly squeeze at the top.
Lower the bar under control along the same path, allowing the elbows to move forward until the arms are fully extended, keeping the bar close to the body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while avoiding torso swing, shoulder elevation, or using momentum to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A standing curl variation where the bar is pulled up close to the body while the elbows move back (dragging the bar along the torso) to emphasize the biceps peak and long head. The movement reduces shoulder flexion and encourages a more vertical forearm path.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Double Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down between your legs and palms facing up (supinated grip).
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with feet flat, legs spread wide and knees bent so your inner thighs can act as a brace; lean forward slightly at the hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms fully extended down between your legs and palms facing up (supinated grip).
Press your upper arms/elbows firmly against your inner thighs near the knees to prevent elbow movement throughout the set.
Exhale and curl both dumbbells simultaneously by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and wrists neutral; bring the dumbbells toward your shoulders and squeeze the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the fully extended starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding swinging the torso or lifting the elbows off the thighs.
exercises.description: A seated, single-position curl where both dumbbells are curled simultaneously with the elbows braced against the inner thighs to isolate the biceps. Performed by leaning forward on a bench and curling the weights up while keeping the upper arms stationary.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Lean forward slightly and rest the working elbow on the inside of the same-side thigh just above the knee; let the arm hang fully extended (upright, seated position).
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and legs spread; pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a supinated (palm-up) grip.
Lean forward slightly and rest the working elbow on the inside of the same-side thigh just above the knee; let the arm hang fully extended (upright, seated position).

Brace your core, keep your upper arm and torso still, then curl the dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is near your shoulder; squeeze at the top.
Pause 1 second at the top, then lower the dumbbell slowly and under control back to the fully extended starting position — avoid letting the wrist collapse or the shoulder help.
Perform the desired number of reps with strict form, then switch sides. Breathe out on the curl (concentric) and inhale on the lowering (eccentric).
exercises.description: A seated, single-arm dumbbell curl that isolates the biceps by bracing the elbow against the inner thigh to minimize shoulder and torso involvement. It emphasizes a slow, controlled concentric and eccentric motion for maximum biceps contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
