exercises.name: Dumbbell High Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart and core engaged, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
instructions: Stand upright, feet hip-width apart and core engaged, holding a dumbbell in each hand.

Raise your upper arms out to the sides so they are approximately parallel to the floor (elbows at shoulder height) with a slight bend at the elbow; palms should face forward (supinated) or slightly inward depending on comfort.
Keep your torso still and shoulders down; do not lean or swing.
Curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbows, bringing the weights up toward your head/ear level while keeping the upper arms fixed in the lateral position; exhale as you lift.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the start position with control while inhaling, maintaining the upper-arm position and neutral wrists.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: A standing curl variation performed with the upper arms held out to the sides so the elbows are at or near shoulder height; the movement emphasizes elbow flexion to load the biceps. Perform controlled curls up toward the head, pause at the top, then lower with control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Close grip Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) close grip so the dumbbells are touching or nearly touching over your sternum.
Tuck your elbows close to your sides (keep them pointing rearward, not flared wide) to bias the triceps.

Brace your core and inhale, then press the dumbbells straight up by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight and triceps are contracted; keep the dumbbells close together throughout the movement.
Pause briefly at the top without locking the elbows aggressively, maintaining wrist neutrality.
Exhale and slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the starting position at the chest while maintaining elbow tuck and shoulder stability.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement tempo controlled and avoiding excessive shoulder flaring or back drive.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a flat bench, press two dumbbells together with a narrow/neutral grip and extend the arms to emphasize the triceps while still recruiting the chest and front delts. Keeping the dumbbells close limits shoulder flare and shifts load onto the triceps.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Close Grip Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Set the preacher bench height so your armpits/upper arms rest comfortably on the pad and your chest is supported while seated.
instructions: Set the preacher bench height so your armpits/upper arms rest comfortably on the pad and your chest is supported while seated.
Load an EZ bar with an appropriate weight and take a close (narrow) underhand grip on the inner bends of the bar, hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Sit with your feet flat on the floor, chest against the pad and upper arms lying flat on the preacher pad; let your arms hang down fully extended to start.
Brace your core, keep your torso stable and wrists neutral, then exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows until your forearms approach vertical and biceps are fully contracted.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the biceps, then inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control until your arms are fully extended and the biceps are lengthened.
Keep the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoid rocking the torso or using momentum; repeat for the desired number of reps.
exercises.description: Seated on a preacher bench, the EZ bar close-grip preacher curl isolates the biceps by preventing upper-arm movement and forcing the elbow to do the work. Using a narrow grip places additional emphasis on the biceps peak while engaging the forearms for grip and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Kickback
exercises.notes: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent and torso leaning forward at the hips (chest toward thighs) so your back is nearly parallel to the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Let your upper arms hang down close to your sides with elbows bent ~90°.
Brace your core and keep your neck neutral; keep the upper arms fixed against your torso (avoid swinging the shoulders).
Exhale and extend the forearms straight back by contracting the triceps until the elbows are fully extended and the dumbbells are behind you; keep the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, squeezing the triceps, then inhale and slowly lower the weights back to the starting 90° elbow bend with controlled motion.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a rigid torso and stationary upper arms throughout each repetition. Avoid using momentum or letting the elbows drift forward.
exercises.description: Seated triceps kickbacks are performed from a forward-leaning seated position, extending the forearm back to isolate and work the triceps. Keep the upper arm stationary and move only at the elbow to emphasize the posterior arm.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; grip the SZ bar with an underhand (palms up) reverse grip roughly shoulder-width apart.
Tuck your elbows close to your sides and keep your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and extend your elbows to push the bar down until your arms are nearly straight and triceps are fully contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid flaring the elbows.
Pause briefly at the bottom to feel the triceps contraction without locking the elbows or using torso momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, returning to about a 90-degree elbow angle or slightly less while maintaining elbow position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement tempo controlled and avoiding shoulder or torso involvement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip to emphasize elbow extension and load the triceps. Perform controlled extensions and returns while keeping the upper arms stationary to isolate the triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Alternate Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (arms fully extended) while seated.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a neutral spine; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (arms fully extended) while seated.
Start with palms facing inward (neutral grip) and elbows close to your torso; keep the upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl one dumbbell by flexing the elbow while rotating (supinating) the wrist so the palm faces your shoulder at the top of the movement; pause and squeeze the biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position with control, returning the wrist to the neutral position if desired.
Repeat the curl with the opposite arm, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions; avoid swinging the torso or using shoulder momentum.
Maintain a steady tempo, keep elbows fixed near your ribs, and breathe out on the concentric (curl) and in on the eccentric (lower).
exercises.description: Seated alternate dumbbell biceps curl performed one arm at a time to isolate the biceps while minimizing body swing. Each repetition includes a curl with wrist supination and a controlled eccentric return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand upright (neutral spine) with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
Brace your abs and pull your shoulder blades down and back so the chest stays tall throughout the movement.
Initiate the movement by sending the hips back as if sitting onto a chair, bending at the knees and hips simultaneously.
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and avoid letting them collapse inward; maintain weight through the midfoot/heels.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or as far as your mobility safely allows) while keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the quads and glutes.
Drive through your heels and extend hips and knees to return to standing, exhaling as you rise and squeezing the glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, controlling the descent and avoiding bouncing at the bottom.
exercises.description: A standing resistance exercise where you lower your hips into a squat while holding dumbbells at your sides to load the legs and hips, primarily working the quads. Maintain a neutral spine and braced core as you descend and drive back up through the heels.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
Stand with the balls of your feet on a raised platform, plate, or step so your heels can drop below the platform; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unrack the bar and step clear of the rack, keeping your torso tall, chest up and core braced.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, keeping knees straight but not locked and avoiding forward lean.
Pause and squeeze the calves at the top for a second.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent—do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired reps, then carefully step back into the rack to re-rack the bar.
Common cues: keep a neutral spine, maintain steady tempo, breathe out on the lift and in on the lower, and use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raises are a weighted heel-raise exercise performed with a barbell across the upper back to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). The movement raises the heels up onto the balls of the feet and then lowers back down for a full calf contraction and stretch.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the machine for a lying (prone) single-leg curl: adjust the ankle pad so it sits just above the heel when your knee is aligned with the machine pivot.
instructions: Set the machine for a lying (prone) single-leg curl: adjust the ankle pad so it sits just above the heel when your knee is aligned with the machine pivot.
Lie prone on the bench with hips and pelvis flat on the pad, chest supported, and one ankle positioned under the lever pad; grip the front handles or bench for stability.
Keep your torso and hips pressed into the bench (avoid lifting or rotating the hips) and the non-working leg relaxed or slightly bent off the pad.
Exhale and curl the working lower leg by flexing the knee, pulling the pad toward your glutes in a controlled motion; focus on contracting the hamstrings.
Pause briefly and squeeze the hamstrings at the top without jerking or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position until the knee is near full extension, maintaining hip contact with the bench.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat, maintaining controlled tempo and full range of motion throughout.
exercises.description: Prone single-leg curl performed on a lever machine where you curl one lower leg upward against a pad to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings. Performed one leg at a time to reduce compensation and increase unilateral control.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and fasten it securely around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and fasten it securely around the working ankle.

Stand facing the machine, step back so the cable has light tension, hinge at the hips and lean forward, resting your forearms or hands on the cable frame or a support; keep a slight bend in the standing knee.
Set your spine neutral (no excessive rounding or arching) and brace your core (abs) to stabilize the trunk.
Initiate the movement by driving the working heel backward and slightly upward through the hip, extending the hip while keeping the knee softly bent and the pelvis square to the floor.
Squeeze the glute at the top of the movement and avoid rotating or tilting the pelvis; keep the torso stationary against the support.
Slowly control the cable as you return the leg to the start position until tension is maintained but the foot does not touch the floor (or returns lightly), maintaining core stability.
Breathe out during the hip extension and inhale during the return. Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides.
Use a full, controlled range of motion with a weight that allows you to maintain a neutral spine and avoid momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: A cable-resisted hip extension performed from a bent-over, supported position that isolates the glute muscles by driving the working heel backward and slightly upward against the cable. The non-working leg remains on the floor for balance while the torso is braced on the machine.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift from Deficit
exercises.notes: Stand on a stable raised platform or plate (the deficit) with feet about hip-width and the bar over mid-foot.
instructions: Stand on a stable raised platform or plate (the deficit) with feet about hip-width and the bar over mid-foot.
Push hips back and bend knees to reach the bar; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or mixed grip, keeping shoulders slightly in front of the bar.
Set a neutral spine and braced core, pulling the shoulder blades slightly down and back to engage the upper back and lats.
Drive through the heels and extend the hips and knees simultaneously, keeping the bar close to the shins/thighs as you stand up to full hip extension (lockout).
Squeeze the glutes at the top without hyperextending the lower back; maintain a tall chest and engaged upper back/traps.
Lower the bar with control by hinging at the hips and bending the knees, returning the bar to the floor under control while maintaining a neutral spine and steady grip.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift performed while standing on a small platform (deficit) to increase range of motion; hinge at the hips and extend to lift the bar from the floor, emphasizing hip drive and posterior chain engagement.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest so your hips and knees align with the machine's pivot; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back fully supported against the back pad (seated position) and grip the handles for stability.
Position the lower leg pad just above your heels / at the lower calf (behind the ankles) and secure the thigh pad across your front upper thighs to prevent hip movement.
Start with your legs nearly extended (knees close to straight but not locked) and feet pointed forward.
Exhale and curl your lower legs downward/back by flexing the knees, bringing the pad toward the underside of your thighs; contract the hamstrings and glutes at the peak.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, then inhale and slowly extend your knees to return the weight to the start position with controlled tempo—do not let the stack slam or lock the knees.
Keep your hips and torso stable against the pad throughout; avoid lifting the hips or using momentum to move the weight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and adjust weight if you cannot maintain smooth, controlled reps.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl performed on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to target the hamstrings while the seat/back pad stabilizes the hips. The movement curls the lower leg back toward the underside of the thigh and then returns under control to the start.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
Position feet about hip-width apart and keep a slight, natural bend in the knees (do not lock the knees).
Engage your abs to stabilize the torso and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet until you are fully on your toes and calves are fully contracted; avoid rocking the torso forward.
Pause for 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the step level until you feel a stretch in the calves (control the descent — do not drop abruptly).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and maintaining balance; if needed, reduce weight or use a rail/wall for light support.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed on a raised step while holding dumbbells at the sides to load the lower leg; the movement raises the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). Emphasis is on controlled concentric raise and slow eccentric lowering below the step for stretch.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Stand in a wide (sumo) stance with feet wider than shoulder-width and toes turned slightly outward; hold a single dumbbell vertically with both hands between your legs, arms extended.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Staggered Stance Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with a staggered stance: one foot slightly forward and the other foot slightly back (back heel can be raised); feet about hip-width apart side-to-side.

exercises.name: Smith Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to just below mid‑shin height and load an appropriate weight; lock safety stops if available.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Plyo Squat
exercises.notes: Stand in a wide (sumo) stance with feet wider than shoulder-width and toes turned slightly outward; hold a single dumbbell vertically with both hands between your legs, arms extended.
instructions: Stand in a wide (sumo) stance with feet wider than shoulder-width and toes turned slightly outward; hold a single dumbbell vertically with both hands between your legs, arms extended.

Brace your core, keep chest up and eyes forward, and initiate the movement by pushing hips back slightly and bending at the knees into a controlled squat until thighs are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining a neutral spine and knees tracking over toes.
Explode upward powerfully through the feet into a small vertical jump, extending hips and knees fully while holding the dumbbell steady between your legs.
Land softly on the midfoot with slightly bent knees to absorb impact, immediately returning into the squat position (do not lock knees at the top).
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled landings and consistent posture throughout; reduce jump height or use bodyweight if technique or landing control is compromised.
exercises.description: A weighted plyometric (jump) squat performed from a wide/sumo stance while holding a dumbbell vertically between the hands; the movement develops explosive leg power and primarily targets the quadriceps while engaging the posterior chain and core for stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Staggered Stance Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with a staggered stance: one foot slightly forward and the other foot slightly back (back heel can be raised); feet about hip-width apart side-to-side.
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with a staggered stance: one foot slightly forward and the other foot slightly back (back heel can be raised); feet about hip-width apart side-to-side.
Keep a slight bend in both knees (soft knees), chest up, shoulders back and core engaged to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Begin the movement by hinging at the hips: push your hips straight back while keeping the dumbbells close to the front leg, lowering them toward mid-shin until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Avoid rounding the lower back—maintain a long spine and neutral neck; eyes should look a few feet forward to help keep the spine aligned.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the hinge (when hamstrings are loaded), then drive the hips forward by contracting the hamstrings and glutes to return to the starting upright position.
Keep the weight distributed through the heel/whole foot of the front leg for stability and control; maintain even tension in the back leg for balance.
Repeat for desired reps, then switch which foot is forward to work both sides evenly.
exercises.description: A Romanian deadlift performed from a staggered (split) stance using dumbbells that emphasizes a hip-hinge to load the hamstrings while the staggered position increases unilateral demand and glute activation. Maintain a neutral spine and soft knees while lowering the weights close to the legs.

exercises.name: Smith Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to just below mid‑shin height and load an appropriate weight; lock safety stops if available.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to just below mid‑shin height and load an appropriate weight; lock safety stops if available.
Stand facing the bar with feet about hip‑width apart and the bar positioned over the middle of your feet; adopt a standing position (not kneeling or prone).
Bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar, keeping a neutral spine and chest lifted; grip the bar with an overhand or mixed grip just outside your knees.
Initiate the lift by driving through your heels and extending your hips, keeping the bar close to your legs and maintaining a straight, braced torso.
Fully extend the hips at the top and squeeze the glutes briefly without overarching the lower back; exhale as you complete the upward phase.
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips and allowing the knees to bend slightly until the bar returns to the starting position near mid‑shin.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a neutral spine and steady tempo; rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: Smith machine deadlift is a hip‑hinge pulling exercise performed on a guided bar that primarily develops the glutes and posterior chain while training hip extension and back stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
Stand with the balls of your feet on a raised platform, plate, or step so your heels can drop below the platform; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unrack the bar and step clear of the rack, keeping your torso tall, chest up and core braced.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, keeping knees straight but not locked and avoiding forward lean.
Pause and squeeze the calves at the top for a second.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent—do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired reps, then carefully step back into the rack to re-rack the bar.
Common cues: keep a neutral spine, maintain steady tempo, breathe out on the lift and in on the lower, and use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raises are a weighted heel-raise exercise performed with a barbell across the upper back to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). The movement raises the heels up onto the balls of the feet and then lowers back down for a full calf contraction and stretch.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your back flat against the pad, chest up, and grip the side handles for stability; keep the non-working leg relaxed on the floor or slightly bent.
Start with the working knee bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and the foot dorsiflexed (toes up) so the pad contacts above the ankle.
Exhale and extend the working knee by lifting the lower leg until it is almost straight; avoid fully locking the knee at the top.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize quad contraction while maintaining a controlled posture (abs engaged, back against pad).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it drop.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions, then switch sides and perform the same steps for the other leg.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg extension on a lever machine that isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to focus on each quad and correct imbalances.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Single Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to a low pulley on a cable machine and fasten it securely around one ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low pulley on a cable machine and fasten it securely around one ankle.
Sit upright on a bench (slightly inclined or upright) facing away from the machine so the cable pulls across the front of the knee; brace your torso and place hands on the bench or pad for support.
Start with the working knee bent ~90 degrees and foot relaxed, keeping the thigh pressed against the seat.
Exhale and extend the knee by contracting the quad until the leg is nearly straight (avoid fully locking the knee).
Hold the top position briefly with controlled tension in the quad.
Inhale and slowly lower the foot back to the start position, resisting the cable for a controlled eccentric phase.
Complete the desired reps, then switch legs and repeat; maintain an upright seated posture and keep hips stationary throughout.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg cable extension isolates knee extension to work one quadriceps at a time using a low-pulley cable and ankle cuff. It emphasizes controlled concentric extension and slow eccentric return for quad strength and balance.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Static Lunge
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart while holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart while holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
Take a long step back with one foot and settle into a staggered, split stance so both feet point forward; keep the front foot flat and the rear heel lifted on the toes.
Brace your core, keep your chest tall and shoulders relaxed, and look forward.
Slowly bend both knees to lower your body straight down until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor and the rear knee is just above the ground (about 90° at the front knee).
Ensure the front knee tracks over the toes (does not collapse inward) and does not travel excessively past the toes.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Drive through the front heel to press the body back up to the starting split-stance position.
Perform the desired number of repetitions while keeping both feet planted (do not step forward or backward between reps), then switch legs and repeat.
Breathe in as you lower, exhale as you push up; maintain a neutral spine and avoid leaning the torso forward throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A stationary split-stance lunge performed while holding dumbbells at your sides; you lower the back knee toward the floor and drive back up without stepping the feet. It emphasizes the front thigh (quadriceps) while the glutes, hamstrings, calves and core stabilize and assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift from Blocks
exercises.notes: Set two sturdy blocks under the barbell so the bar rests elevated (typically 2–6 inches off the floor) and load the desired weight.
instructions: Set two sturdy blocks under the barbell so the bar rests elevated (typically 2–6 inches off the floor) and load the desired weight.
Stand with feet about hip-width apart with toes pointing forward or slightly out; position the bar over the middle of your feet so it sits close to your shins.
Hinge at the hips and push your butt back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip.
Set a neutral spine: chest up, shoulders slightly in front of the bar, shoulder blades down and back — do not round the lower back.
Take a full breath and brace your abs, then drive the feet into the floor and extend the hips forward aggressively to lift the bar, keeping it close to your legs.
Stand fully upright at the top by squeezing the glutes and locking out the hips; avoid excessive leaning back.
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips first, then bending the knees as needed, and place the bar back onto the blocks before repeating.
Keep consistent breathing and core bracing; stop the set if you cannot maintain a neutral spine or if grip fails.
exercises.description: A barbell deadlift started from elevated blocks that increases range of motion and emphasizes hip extension; the lift primarily drives the hips forward to extend the body from a hinged position. It trains the posterior chain while also engaging the legs, back and grip.

exercises.name: Lever Seated One Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat and back pad so your thigh is supported and the rotating lever pad rests just above your ankle when the leg is extended; select an appropriate weight.

Sit upright with your back pressed firmly against the back pad, chest up, and grasp the handles or seat grips for stability; position the non-working foot flat on the floor or on the machine footrest.
Start with the working leg extended (knee nearly straight) and the ankle neutral; this is the starting position.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knee, driving the heel toward the underside of your glute while keeping the thigh and hips stationary against the seat.
Pause briefly at the top of the contraction and squeeze the hamstrings; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips.
Inhale and slowly return the lower leg to the starting extended position under control, resisting the weight on the way down.
Complete all reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg. Maintain a controlled tempo and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Seated single‑leg curl performed on a lever machine to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to correct imbalances and increase muscle activation.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
Position your shoulders under the padded lever, stand tall and grip the machine handles for stability; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward (or slightly turned in/out to vary emphasis).
Begin with a full stretch: lower your heels down until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves (keep torso upright and spine neutral).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, exhaling and keeping knees straight (a slight knee bend shifts emphasis to the soleus if desired).
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched starting position with control—avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and proper alignment throughout.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing calf raise where the shoulders are braced under a padded lever and the heels drop and rise from a platform to load the calf muscles through a full range of motion. Emphasizes plantarflexion to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curtsey Lunge
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
Brace your abs and keep your chest tall and shoulders back throughout the movement.

Step your right leg diagonally behind and across your left leg (as if performing a curtsy) so your right foot lands behind and slightly outside the left foot; maintain weight in the left (front) heel.
Lower your body by bending both knees until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor and the back knee is approaching the floor, keeping hips level and torso upright.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press through the front heel and glute of the working leg to return to the starting standing position.
Repeat for the desired reps on one side, then switch legs and perform the same number on the other side.
Coaching cues: keep the front knee tracking over the toes (not collapsing inward), avoid leaning forward, control the step and return rather than bouncing.
exercises.description: A single-leg lunge variation where you step the rear leg diagonally behind the front leg (a 'curtsy') while holding dumbbells; it emphasizes the glutes and inner thighs while also working the quads and hamstrings. Performed slowly with control to improve unilateral strength and hip stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Straight Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at arm's length in front of your thighs (palms facing your body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at arm's length in front of your thighs (palms facing your body).
Soften (do not lock) your knees with a slight bend and engage your core to stabilize the spine.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) while keeping the chest lifted and a neutral spine and neck.

Lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner, keeping them close to your legs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (roughly to mid-shin or just below the knees), avoiding any rounding of the lower back.
Keep your weight in your heels and maintain constant tension in the hamstrings; do not bend excessively at the knees.
Reverse the movement by driving the hips forward, squeezing the glutes at the top, and returning to a tall standing position while exhaling.
Repeat for desired reps with controlled tempo and maintain proper form throughout (neutral spine, hips driving the motion, and firm grip on the dumbbells).
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with dumbbells that places primary emphasis on the hamstrings while also loading the glutes, lower back and grip. The exercise involves keeping the legs mostly straight (soft knees) and bending at the hips to lower the weights along the legs.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet hip-width apart on an elevated surface (plate or block) so heels can drop below the toes.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet hip-width apart on an elevated surface (plate or block) so heels can drop below the toes.
Place one or two dumbbells vertically across your thighs just above the knees and hold them steady with your hands to keep them from rolling.
Keep your torso upright, core braced, and knees stationary throughout the movement.
Start with heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Push through the balls of both feet and raise your heels as high as possible (plantarflexion) to fully contract the calves; hold for 1 second at the top.
Slowly lower the heels back down below the level of the elevated platform until you feel a controlled stretch; avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining full range of motion and controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Seated calf raise performed with dumbbells places load on the soles of the feet while seated, emphasizing the calf muscles (especially the soleus). The movement raises and lowers the heels through plantarflexion while knees remain bent.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with heels hanging off the edge; grip the handles at your sides for stability.
Start with your ankles dorsiflexed (heels lowered) to feel a stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortably possible while maintaining control.
If the machine allows rotary motion, rotate the footplate slightly inward or outward across sets or reps to emphasize medial or lateral calf fibers while maintaining the same plantarflexion pattern.
Pause briefly at the top for maximal contraction, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back to the starting stretched position with a controlled tempo.
Keep the movement smooth, avoid bouncing, and maintain a stable seated posture (do not lift hips or lock knees).
exercises.description: Seated lever rotary calf is a seated plantarflexion exercise performed on a lever/rotary calf machine that isolates the calf muscles through a full range of motion, often allowing a slight rotary/angle change at the footplate to target different calf fibers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside the Smith machine so it sits behind you at a height that allows your hips to lightly touch the bench at the bottom of the squat without fully relaxing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge from Deficit
exercises.notes: Start standing upright on a low step or platform with your front foot centered on the platform and the rear foot on the floor behind you; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and brace your core.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.

exercises.name: Smith Bent Knee Good morning
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; position the safety stops so you can re-rack safely.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.

exercises.name: Smith Squat
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside the Smith machine so it sits behind you at a height that allows your hips to lightly touch the bench at the bottom of the squat without fully relaxing.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside the Smith machine so it sits behind you at a height that allows your hips to lightly touch the bench at the bottom of the squat without fully relaxing.

Load the bar, step under it and position the bar across your upper traps/trapezius (not on the neck). Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width, feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Unrack the bar by rotating it and take one or two small steps forward so the bench is directly behind you; maintain a neutral spine and an upright chest.
Inhale and initiate the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, keeping weight through your heels and mid-foot and ensuring your knees track over your toes.
Lower under control until your glutes lightly touch the bench (brief contact only) — do not sit down fully or bounce off the bench.
Pause briefly, then exhale and drive through your heels to stand by extending the knees and hips until fully upright without hyperextending the lower back.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then carefully step forward and re-rack the bar by rotating it onto the Smith machine catches.
Use safety stops at an appropriate height, keep a controlled tempo, and adjust foot position (slightly forward or neutral) to vary quad vs. glute emphasis as needed.
exercises.description: A bench-assisted squat performed in a Smith machine that emphasizes the quadriceps by sitting back lightly to a bench and then standing back up under controlled tension. The bench provides a consistent depth cue and reduces balance demand so you can focus on quad drive.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge from Deficit
exercises.notes: Start standing upright on a low step or platform with your front foot centered on the platform and the rear foot on the floor behind you; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and brace your core.
instructions: Start standing upright on a low step or platform with your front foot centered on the platform and the rear foot on the floor behind you; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and brace your core.
Keep your torso tall and shoulders back, then step the rear foot straight back off the platform into a controlled reverse lunge so the front knee bends and the back knee lowers toward the floor.
Lower until the front thigh is about parallel to the floor or you feel a full comfortable stretch (front knee tracking over toes), keeping most of your weight through the front heel.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and return the rear foot up onto the platform to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps on one leg, then switch legs; maintain a controlled tempo, full range of motion from the deficit, and keep the torso upright throughout.
exercises.description: A single-leg reverse lunge performed while the front foot is positioned on a raised platform (deficit) to increase range of motion; dumbbells are held at the sides for added resistance. The exercise emphasizes knee extension of the front leg while challenging unilateral strength and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright (single-leg stance) on the working foot with toes facing forward; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred) and let the non-working leg hang or be slightly bent behind you -- torso neutral and shoulders relaxed.
Find balance and keep the knee of the working leg straight or with a slight bend (do not lock); engage your abs to stabilize the torso and keep weight centered over the midfoot.
Slowly raise your heel by plantarflexing the ankle (push through the ball of the foot) until you reach maximum height on the toes; avoid leaning forward or using momentum.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calf, maintaining an upright posture and stable core.
Lower the heel slowly and with control until the sole is flat on the floor (or slightly below the step if performed on an elevated surface) to complete one repetition.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch legs; use a nearby wall or rail for light support if balance is a limiting factor but avoid pushing off with the support.
exercises.description: A single-leg calf raise performed standing while holding dumbbell(s) to add resistance; it isolates the calf (gastrocnemius/soleus) on the working leg while the opposite leg is off the ground. Balance and core stability are required to perform controlled raises and lowers.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Smith Bent Knee Good morning
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; position the safety stops so you can re-rack safely.
instructions: Set the Smith bar at shoulder height and load appropriate weight; position the safety stops so you can re-rack safely.
Stand under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps, grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, then step back to stand with feet about shoulder-width apart.
Bend the knees slightly (soft knees) and brace your core; keep a neutral spine and chest lifted throughout the movement.

Begin the rep by pushing the hips straight back (hip hinge) while maintaining the slight knee bend; allow the torso to lean forward until you feel a controlled stretch in the glutes/hamstrings or your torso is roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the bar travel vertical on the Smith track and maintain a strong, neutral spine — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes to return to the upright starting position, keeping the motion controlled.
Exhale as you return to standing and keep the knees slightly flexed throughout; do not turn the movement into a squat (minimize knee travel forward).
After completing the set, carefully walk the bar forward and re-rack it on the Smith machine using the safety catches if needed.
exercises.description: A bent-knee good morning performed on a Smith machine: the lifter hinges at the hips with a bar on the upper back to load the glutes and spinal erectors while keeping the knees slightly bent. The movement emphasizes hip extension and posterior chain control.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Fly
exercises.notes: Set the seat height so the handles are at mid-chest level when seated; select an appropriate weight and sit with your back firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set the seat height so the handles are at mid-chest level when seated; select an appropriate weight and sit with your back firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip, open your chest, keep a slight bend in the elbows, and position your upper arms roughly parallel to the floor (seated, upright posture).
Exhale and squeeze your pectorals to bring the handles together in a controlled arc until your hands/handles meet or nearly meet in front of your chest; avoid using momentum.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, focusing on a chest squeeze rather than pressing with the arms or shoulders.
Inhale and slowly allow the handles to return to the starting wide position under control, maintaining the slight elbow bend and keeping your back against the pad.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid locking the elbows, leaning forward, or shrugging the shoulders during the movement.
exercises.description: Seated lever fly is a machine chest fly that isolates the pectoral muscles by bringing the arms together in a horizontal arc from an open-arm start position while seated with the back supported. It emphasizes a controlled concentric squeeze of the chest and a slow eccentric return.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.

Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
